beats.activity: Wake up, coffee, light breakfast, watch rain from window; Check weather alerts, decide routing strategy for Renton
beats.reasoning: Low energy (40/100) from yesterday means a slower morning; rainy Tuesday invites staying cozy indoors. Coffee ritual centers the day.

beats.activity: Drive to Renton via I-405 south, avoiding SR-520 closure
beats.reasoning: Rain and SR-520 closure force longer southern route (I-90 to I-405); budget 35 min for wet conditions and detour.

beats.activity: Teach first guitar lesson (beginner student); Teach second guitar lesson (intermediate student); Teach third guitar lesson (advanced student)
beats.reasoning: Early lesson slot; yesterday's success with fingerpicking student energizes Harper to show up focused despite low morning energy.

beats.activity: Grab lunch at a casual diner, wait out the tail end of rain
beats.reasoning: Rain peaks during this window; Harper takes shelter, refuels, and lets the worst weather pass before heading back north.

beats.activity: Drive back to Seattle via I-405/I-90, rain clearing
beats.reasoning: SR-520 closure ends at noon; I-90 is clear. Heading to Capitol Hill pottery studio for afternoon session aligns with spontaneous mood and creative outlet.

beats.activity: Work at pottery studio, throwing and glazing
beats.reasoning: Rain ends by 2pm; Harper gets hands in clay, a grounding ritual after morning's teaching hustle. Low energy → introspective, solitary creative time.

beats.activity: Walk to camera shop, browse new film stock
beats.reasoning: Within Capitol Hill neighborhood; spontaneous impulse to feed photography interest after pottery. Clears sky by afternoon, good walking weather.

beats.activity: Meet friend for coffee and conversation at a cozy café
beats.reasoning: Harper values close friends over crowds; repeating yesterday's easy café ritual but earlier, while energy is still available. Low-stakes, restorative.

beats.activity: Commute home, stop at grocery for dinner ingredients
beats.reasoning: Quick errand-commute hybrid; grab simple ingredients for a quiet dinner at home, wind down after a full day.

beats.activity: Cook dinner, practice guitar, organize photos, wind down
beats.reasoning: Quiet evening at home mirrors yesterday's balanced end; low energy means early bedtime. Solitary creative time (guitar, photos) before sleep.
7-hour sleep block ending at 6am next day; low energy justifies longer rest to recharge for Wednesday.

diary: Woke to that grey Seattle drizzle and immediately checked the traffic alerts—SR-520 was a mess, so I took I-405 south to Renton instead. Three back-to-back guitar lessons kept me sharp; there's something about moving from a beginner fumbling through their first chord changes to an advanced student nailing a tricky fingerpicking pattern that reminds me why I love this work. Grabbed lunch at Denny's and watched the rain taper off through the window. By the time I got back to Capitol Hill, the clouds were breaking up. Spent a few hours at the pottery studio throwing clay—something about the wheel's rhythm after teaching all morning felt necessary. Stopped by Glazos to check their new Portra stock, then met Sam at Café Solstice. We talked for an hour, which turned into the kind of conversation you don't plan for but desperately need. Drove home as the sun tried to peek through, picked up groceries, cooked something simple. Now my hands smell like clay and garlic, and I'm sitting with my guitar in the quiet apartment. This day felt balanced somehow.
thought_process: The bridge closure could've derailed everything, but I'd already mapped out the I-405 route before leaving—I've learned to check those alerts first thing. Teaching three levels back-to-back usually drains me, but today it felt energizing; each student brought something different. I think the rain's mood was contagious early on, made me want to move slow. The pottery studio was deliberate—after an intense morning, I needed something tactile and meditative. When Sam texted about coffee, I almost declined (I could've gone straight home), but I'm glad I didn't. Sometimes you need to be around people who get you. By evening, the weather had lifted my spirits along with the clouds, and I felt creative enough to actually practice instead of just noodle around. The day had rhythm to it, despite the chaos on the highways.
world_event_prompt: Grey, drizzly Tuesday with steady rain from 7am to 2pm, high of 58F. SR-520 bridge closed eastbound 9am-noon for emergency repair, snarling cross-lake traffic.
